mnemonics: A Full Adder is a basic building block for half adders
A Subtractor is essential for binary arithmetic operations
keyPoints: A 4-bit Full Adder consists of 4 half adders and 2 carry-in lines
The 4-bit Full Adder can be simulated using basic gates like AND, OR, and NOT
The 4-bit Subtractor can be simulated using basic gates like AND, OR, and NOT along with an inverter
The 4-bit Full Adder has 4 inputs (A, B, Cin, and Sum) and 3 outputs (Sum, Cout, and Carry)
The 4-bit Subtractor has 4 inputs (A, B, Bin, and Cin) and 3 outputs (Sum, Cout, and Carry)
A 4-bit Full Adder can be designed using the following logic gates: 2x AND, 2x OR, and 4x NOT
A 4-bit Subtractor can be designed using the following logic gates: 2x AND, 2x OR, 2x NOT, and 1x XOR
